domain: Giving AI coding agents good taste in frontend design using the open-source Taste Skill framework by Leon Lin (anti-slop SKILL.md ruleset: three dials, audit-first redesigns, image-first pipeline, anti-laziness rules, strict pre-flight check).
refresh_cadence: on-changelog for github.com/Leonxlnx/taste-skill and tasteskill.dev/changelog; monthly for the SKILL.md rules and the three dials; quarterly for ecosystem coverage (Emil Kowalski, Developers Digest, skill directories) and the Anthropic Agent Skills / SKILL.md format

researched_requires: at least two captured sources for fast-moving or high-stakes domains
at least one official/primary source for lower-risk domains
retrieved dates, refresh_due dates, source_type, confidence, and claim coverage

sources.title: Taste Skill - canonical v2 SKILL.md (design-taste-frontend)
claims: The three dials DESIGN_VARIANCE / MOTION_INTENSITY / VISUAL_DENSITY use a 1-10 scale with baseline 8 / 6 / 4, set conversationally not by editing the file
Em-dash (U+2014) and en-dash (U+2013) are banned anywhere visible; only the hyphen and math minus are allowed
Hero: headline max 2 lines, subtext max 20 words, CTA visible without scroll, pt-24 top-padding cap, max 4 text elements
Navigation renders on a single line at desktop with an 80px height cap (default 64-72px)
An 8-section page must use at least 4 different layout families; bento grids use exactly N cells for N items
Color/Shape/Page-Theme locks: one accent, one radius scale, one theme per page; premium-consumer beige+brass palette is banned by explicit hex
Section 14 is a mandatory pre-flight check; any failed box blocks completion
Section 11 redesign protocol defines Greenfield / Preserve / Overhaul modes and never-change-silently preservation rules
Scope is landing pages, portfolios, and redesigns; dashboards, data tables, and multi-step product UI are out of scope

sources.title: tasteskill.dev - official site (guide, docs, changelog, llms.txt)
claims: Positioning: 'The Anti-Slop Frontend Framework for AI Agents'; tagline 'Less slop, designs pop.'
Greenfield (Prompt 1) and Redesign (Prompt 2) workflows are published verbatim on the guide
Install: npx skills add https://github.com/Leonxlnx/taste-skill --skill "design-taste-frontend"
llms.txt states the dial scales as 1-10 with endpoints; the guide names the dials without scales
Works with Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Gemini CLI, v0, Lovable, OpenCode via the SKILL.md standard

sources.title: github.com/Leonxlnx/taste-skill - repo metadata, README, CHANGELOG, 13 skills
claims: License is MIT; repo created 2026-02-19; 59,770 (as of 2026-07-07) stars as of 2026-07-06; no git releases or tags
The repo ships 13 skills; the primary install name is design-taste-frontend
research/laziness/ backs the output-skill anti-laziness rules with a documented study
v2 is experimental, a pre-release iterating toward a v2.0.0 stable that will lock install name and dials

sources.title: Anthropic - Equipping agents for the real world with Agent Skills (SKILL.md format, progressive disclosure)
claims: Agent Skills are folders of instructions/scripts/resources agents discover and load dynamically
SKILL.md frontmatter requires at minimum name and description
Progressive disclosure loads name+description at discovery, the full SKILL.md at activation, and bundled files on demand
The format is published as an open standard for cross-platform portability

sources.title: W3C Design Tokens Community Group - first stable specification
claims: A design token expresses design decisions in a platform-agnostic way as a named single source of truth
The specification reached its first stable version in October 2025; token systems cover motion and density, not just color

sources.title: Developers Digest - Taste Skills Are Turning Agent Review Into Infrastructure
claims: A taste skill is a review checklist, style contract, and calibration artifact the agent routes through before claiming work is done
The frontier is better review moved earlier, written down, and reused - not just better generation
Taste Skill sits in a skills-as-infrastructure family with Stop Slop and the Compound Engineering Plugin

sources.title: andrew.ooo - Taste Skill Review (independent)
claims: Taste Skill is the first frontend skill that treats AI slop as a solvable engineering problem rather than a vibes problem
Criticism: the rules are strong opinions (e.g. the Inter ban), examples assume React/Next, and there is no automated test or visual-regression verification

sources.title: neodrop.ai - taste-skill v2: 3 dials and a 60-item preflight check
claims: The v2 rewrite grew from ~500 lines to ~25,000 words across 14 sections
Section 0 Brief Inference forces the agent to commit to a direction before touching layout
The '60-item' pre-flight figure is neodrop's characterization, not stated by the vendor

sources.title: Emil Kowalski - Agents with Taste (and Taste Skill sponsor)
claims: Design taste is articulable logic and can be codified into per-aspect skill files for agents
animations.dev (Emil Kowalski) is an official sponsor of Taste Skill alongside the Vercel OSS Program
His 'Agents with Taste' is a parallel, independent effort, not a review of Leon Lin's project

sources.title: Refactoring UI (Wathan & Schoger) + Butterick's Practical Typography
claims: Visual hierarchy via size/color/weight/placement is the most effective tool for making something feel designed
Default to more whitespace than feels necessary; add density deliberately
Body line length of 45-90 characters underlies the skill's max-w-[65ch] measure rule

sources.title: Why Your AI Keeps Building the Same Purple Gradient Website (AI slop = distributional convergence)
claims: LLMs are statistical pattern matchers that reach for the median of their training corpus, producing AI slop
The default purple traces to Tailwind's demo bg-indigo-500 saturating tutorials and training data
The fix is constraint (forbidden patterns, one direction), not 'make it beautiful' prompts

sources.title: github.com/pbakaus/impeccable - repo metadata, README, 23 commands, 45 detector rules
claims: License is Apache-2.0; repo created 2025-11-16; ~44,115 stars as of 2026-07-07; homepage impeccable.style
Tagline: '1 skill, 23 commands, live browser iteration, and 45 deterministic detector rules for AI-generated frontend design'
Install: npx impeccable install from project root, then /impeccable init inside the AI coding tool
/impeccable init writes PRODUCT.md and offers DESIGN.md covering audience, brand/product lane, voice, anti-references, colors, type, components
Ships one compiled skill plus a standalone detect CLI (npx impeccable detect) and provider-native hook manifests for Claude Code, Copilot, Codex, Cursor
Supported platforms: Cursor, Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Gemini CLI, Codex CLI, OpenCode, Pi, Kiro, Trae, Rovo Dev, Qoder
README states 'Anthropic's frontend-design was the first widely-used design skill for Claude. Impeccable started from there.'
Named anti-slop tells: Inter for everything, purple-to-blue gradients, cards nested in cards

sources.title: impeccable.style - official site (vocabulary positioning, install paths, live mode)
claims: Headline positioning: 'The missing design vocabulary for agents'; shared command vocabulary between humans and agents
Three install paths: /plugin marketplace add pbakaus/impeccable (Claude Code), npx skills add pbakaus/impeccable, npx impeccable install (Node 24+)
23 commands organized by discipline (typography, color, motion, layout); 45 deterministic anti-pattern rules that run without an LLM
Live iteration (beta) swaps three production-quality variants in a running dev server via HMR; edits write directly to source files
Author attribution: Paul Bakaus; a Chrome extension and a CI/CD CLI also exist

sources.title: github.com/pbakaus/impeccable/releases - skill-v/cli-v/ext-v changelog
claims: Latest versions as of 2026-07-07: skill-v3.9.1 and cli-v3.2.0, both published 2026-07-01
cli-v3.2.0 added detector rule #45 (Codex grid-line backgrounds, the two-axis gradient overlay pattern), confirming the 45-rule count
skill-v3.9.0 flags decorative grid backgrounds as anti-patterns, makes /impeccable bolder respect existing design systems, and adds an independent critique mode for non-Claude/Codex platforms
skill-v3.9.1 removed a top-level description field from the plugin hook manifest that Codex's strict hook parser rejected
The project versions the skill, CLI, and Chrome extension separately (skill-v*, cli-v*, ext-v*)

sources.title: github.com/jakubkrehel/make-interfaces-feel-better - repo metadata, no license
claims: Repo created 2026-03-13; ~2,241 stars as of 2026-07-07; last push 2026-04-19; default branch main
The repo has NO license file (GitHub API license: null), so default all-rights-reserved copyright applies
Repo homepage points at the source article jakub.kr/writing/details-that-make-interfaces-feel-better
The skill bundles supporting files typography.md, surfaces.md, animations.md, performance.md alongside SKILL.md

sources.title: make-interfaces-feel-better - canonical SKILL.md (raw)
claims: 16 rule categories including concentric border radius, optical alignment, shadows over borders, interruptible animations, font smoothing, tabular numbers, minimum hit area
Concentric border radius formula: outer radius = inner radius + padding
Button press feedback: scale(0.96), never below 0.95
Interactive elements need a hit area of at least 40x40px, extended with a pseudo-element when the visible element is smaller
Icon animation values: scale 0.25 to 1, opacity 0 to 1, blur 4px to 0; stagger delay ~100ms; Motion spring settings duration 0.3, bounce 0
Image outlines: 1px at 10% opacity (black in light mode, white in dark mode)
Trigger description: use when building UI components, reviewing frontend code, implementing animations, hover states, shadows, borders, typography, micro-interactions, or when interfaces 'feel off'

sources.title: Jakub Krehel - Details That Make Interfaces Feel Better (source article)
claims: The article is the direct source of the skill; Krehel turned the tips into an installable skill after the article's popularity
Animation timing: ~100ms delays for staggered sections, 80ms for individual text spans, 800ms enter durations; exit animations subtler than enter, with 4px blur
Box shadows composed of three layers; text-wrap: balance and text-wrap: pretty; -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums
Krehel publishes 'Interfaces', described as a design engineering magazine; no employer affiliation stated on the article
Krehel shared the article on X on 2026-03-16 (post ID 2033579028288856466 decodes to 2026-03-16T16:19Z); the skill repo was created 2026-03-13

sources.title: anthropics/skills - frontend-design SKILL.md (canonical file, raw)
claims: Frontmatter: name frontend-design; description covers distinctive, intentional visual design, aesthetic direction, typography; license line 'Complete terms in LICENSE.txt'
The sibling LICENSE.txt in the skill folder is the Apache License 2.0
Current file is roughly 1,300-1,400 words across sections: ground it in the subject, design principles, process, restraint and self-critique, writing in design
Names concrete default palettes to avoid: warm cream #F4F1EA with serif display + terracotta accent; near-black with acid-green or vermilion accents; broadsheet hairline-rule layouts
Prescribes a hero thesis, a 4-6 value named hex palette, one justified aesthetic risk, and a two-pass build-critique process
Last commit touching this path: 2235be7c 'Update frontend-design skill (#1293)' on 2026-06-09; prior commits 2025-12-04 and 2025-12-01

sources.title: Anthropic - Improving frontend design through Skills (launch post)
claims: Published 2025-11-12 by Anthropic's Applied AI team (Prithvi Rajasekaran, Justin Wei, Alexander Bricken)
Problem framing: distributional convergence; unguided LLM frontends default to Inter fonts, purple gradients on white, minimal animations because safe choices dominate training data
Four dimensions where targeted prompting works: typography, animations/motion, backgrounds, themes
The launch-era skill was approximately 400 tokens; improvements shown as before/after comparisons on landing pages, blogs, dashboards, with no formal eval methodology detailed
Core principle: the more aesthetic improvements map to implementable frontend code, the better Claude executes
Links the anthropics/skills repo, a Claude Code frontend design plugin, and a frontend aesthetics cookbook

sources.title: github.com/nextlevelbuilder/ui-ux-pro-max-skill - repo, README databases, releases
claims: MIT license; repo created 2025-11-30; ~102,037 stars as of 2026-07-07, the highest-starred of the six covered skills; homepage www.uupm.cc
README counts as of 2026-07-07: 67 UI styles (49 general, 8 landing, 10 BI/dashboard), 161 color palettes, 57 font pairings, 99 UX guidelines plus 161 reasoning rules, 25 chart types, 22 tech stacks
Latest release v2.10.2 published 2026-07-06; eight releases between 2026-06-24 and 2026-07-06
Mechanism: CSV databases searched with a BM25 engine (search.py) across 5 domains in parallel, plus JSON reasoning rules for filtering and ranking
Install: npm install -g ui-ux-pro-max-cli, then uipro init --ai [platform]; v2 flagship is the Design System Generator
18 supported platforms including Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Antigravity, Copilot, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Warp

sources.title: vercel-labs/agent-skills - web-design-guidelines SKILL.md (raw)
claims: Frontmatter metadata: author vercel, version 1.0.0, argument-hint <file-or-pattern>

Workflow: fetch the latest guidelines from raw.githubusercontent.com/vercel-labs/web-interface-guidelines/main/command.md at run time, read target files, check all rules, output terse file:line findings
The skill file itself is roughly 400 words; all rule substance lives in the fetched guidelines document, so reviews always use the current ruleset
Parent repo vercel-labs/agent-skills: 'Vercel's official collection of agent skills', created 2025-12-08, ~28,766 stars as of 2026-07-07, no license file detected by GitHub
Install: npx skills add vercel-labs/agent-skills --skill web-design-guidelines

sources.title: vercel-labs/web-interface-guidelines - command.md ruleset (raw) + vercel.com/design/guidelines
claims: Roughly 90-110 rules across 16+ categories: accessibility, focus, forms, animation, typography, content, images, performance, navigation/state, touch, safe areas, dark mode, i18n, hydration, hover states, copy, anti-patterns

Representative rules: icon-only buttons need aria-label; never outline-none without a focus replacement; never block paste; honor prefers-reduced-motion; virtualize lists over 50 items; URL reflects state; explicit image width/height; Intl.DateTimeFormat for dates; destructive actions need confirm or undo
Prescribes agent output: findings grouped by file in file:line format, terse, pass/fail per file, no preamble
Repo is MIT licensed, created 2025-09-10, ~697 stars as of 2026-07-07; the live page vercel.com/design/guidelines is the human-readable version
The live page credits eight people for feedback, recommends AGENTS.md, and lists agent integrations: Antigravity, Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Windsurf

sources.title: wilwaldon/Claude-Code-Frontend-Design-Toolkit - ecosystem curation list
claims: Curated meta-list: 'Everything I've found that actually makes Claude Code output better-looking frontends. Skills, plugins, MCP servers, CLAUDE.md tricks'
Created 2026-02-25; ~406 stars as of 2026-07-07; no license file detected
Positions Anthropic's frontend-design skill as the baseline that picks an aesthetic direction instead of Inter + purple gradient + rounded cards
Documents the tunable-knob pattern (design variance, motion intensity, visual density) and database-style skills with large style/font/UX-rule counts
Covers the wider workflow ecosystem including Figma MCP design-to-code and the Code to Canvas reverse direction (launched Feb 2026)

sources.title: Snyk - Top 8 Claude Skills for UI/UX Engineers (market snapshot, early 2026)
claims: Lists 8 skills: Anthropic frontend-design, Vercel web design guidelines, Vercel React best practices, Vercel composition patterns, UI/UX Pro Max, Bencium UX designer, AccessLint, Vercel React Native skills; omits taste-skill, Impeccable, and make-interfaces-feel-better
Snapshot numbers: anthropics/skills at 65,847 stars; ui-ux-pro-max at 29,636 stars with 50 styles, 97 palettes, 57 fonts, 25 chart types; Vercel skills at 19,487 stars with 100+ rules
Per-skill last-updated stamps: frontend-design and UI/UX Pro Max February 2026, Vercel skills January 2026; the article itself shows no explicit dateline; byline Stephen Thoemmes
Two direct fetches found NO install counts in this article; the widely circulated ~277k installs figure for frontend-design could not be located here
Cites Snyk ToxicSkills research: prompt injection in 36% of skills tested and 1,467 malicious payloads across the ecosystem; advises reviewing SKILL.md and bundled scripts before installing

sources.title: computertech.co - Impeccable Review 2026 (reception, token overhead anecdote)
claims: Independent review by Sawyer Ruhl, published 2026-03-14; rates Impeccable 8.4/10, 'best-in-class for AI-assisted frontend design', verdict 'get in early'
Token anecdote: 'Loading 7 reference files per session on a $15/million token model adds up'; quantified at approximately 8,000-15,000 tokens of context per session, roughly 10-15% overhead
Snapshot at review time: v1.1 era, 17 slash commands, 7 domain reference files, 1,666+ stars growing ~640 stars/day
Criticisms: opinionated defaults may conflict with existing design systems, early-stage rough edges, variable performance across platforms, not a design-system replacement
The token-overhead observation describes the March 2026 v1.x architecture, not the current v3.x compiled skill

sources.title: Refactoring UI (Adam Wathan and Steve Schoger)
claims: Authors are Adam Wathan and Steve Schoger, the creators of Tailwind CSS; pitch: make your ideas look awesome without relying on a designer
Visual hierarchy via size, color, weight, and placement is the most effective tool for making something feel designed
Default to more whitespace than feels necessary; add density deliberately
Use fewer borders: prefer shadows, color contrast, and spacing to separate elements

sources.title: Butterick's Practical Typography (2nd edition, web book)
claims: Line length rule: 45-90 characters per line, or 2-3 alphabets; measured in characters, not physical width
The 45-90 character measure underlies skill rules like max-w-[65ch] body text limits
Free web-based book by Matthew Butterick, reader-supported and ad-free since 2013, with core chapters on point size, line spacing, and page layout
